perturbation.original_text: Miltenyi will promptly notify Bellicum in writing of Miltenyi's receipt of any written claim or demand from any Third Party alleging that the practice of Miltenyi Technology infringes such Third Party's Intellectual Property Rights, or Miltenyi's receipt of written notice of the initiation of any legal action or other legal proceeding by any Third Party alleging that the practice of Miltenyi Technology infringes such Third Party's Intellectual Property Rights.
perturbation.changed_text: Miltenyi may notify Bellicum of any claim that the practice of Miltenyi Technology infringes a Third Party's Intellectual Property Rights.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'will promptly notify' to 'may notify' removes the mandatory nature of the notification. This introduces legal risk because it prevents Bellicum from being informed of potential IP infringements, which could lead to Bellicum unknowingly infringing on third-party rights and incurring legal liabilities. The original text provided protection by ensuring transparency; the altered version removes this protection.
perturbation.law_explanation: 35 U.S. Code § 287 addresses the limitation on damages and other remedies for patent infringement if the patent holder does not provide notice of the infringement. By making notification optional rather than mandatory, Miltenyi potentially prejudices Bellicum's ability to mitigate damages in case of patent infringement related to Miltenyi Technology used in Bellicum's products. Therefore, the alteration could result in legal contradictions depending on the specific circumstances of potential infringement and damages.

Limitation on damages and other remedies; marking and notice (a) Patentees, and persons making, offering for sale, or selling within the United States any patented article for or under them, or importing any patented article into the United States, may give notice to the public that the same is patented, e
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 35 U.S. Code § 287 - Limitation on damages and other remedies; marking and notice | U.S. Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Quick search by citation: Title Section Go! 35 U.S. Code § 287 - Limitation on damages and other remedies; marking and notice U.S. Code Notes prev | next (a) Patentees, and persons making, offering for sale, or selling within the United States any patented article for or under them, or importing any patented article into the United States , may give notice to the public that the same is patented, either by fixing thereon the word “patent” or the abbreviation “pat.”, together with the number of the patent, or by fixing thereon the word “patent” or the abbreviation “pat.” together with an address of a posting on the Internet, accessible to the public without charge for accessing the address, that associates the patented article with the number of the patent, or when, from the character of the article, this can not be done, by fixing to it, or to the package wherein one or more of them is contained, a label containing a like notice. In the event of failure so to mark, no damages shall be recovered by the patentee in any action for infringement, except on proof that the infringer was notified of the infringement and continued to infringe thereafter, in which event damages may be recovered only for infringement occurring after such notice. Filing of an action for infringement shall constitute such notice. (b) (1) An infringer under section 271(g) shall be subject to all the provisions of this title relating to damages and injunctions except to the extent those remedies are modified by this subsection or section 9006 of the Process Patent Amendments Act of 1988 . The modifications of remedies provided in this subsection shall not be available to any person who— (A) practiced the patented process; (B) owns or controls, or is owned or controlled by, the person who practiced the patented process; or (C) had k
perturbation.justification: The modification, changing 'will promptly notify' to 'may notify', directly contradicts the legal obligation outlined in 35 U.S. Code § 287. While the code doesn't explicitly state that notification must be 'prompt', it emphasizes the importance of notice for patent infringement. The changed text removes the mandatory nature of the notification, potentially prejudicing Bellicum's ability to mitigate damages in case of patent infringement, as mentioned in the Law Explanation. This aligns with the Omission Legal Contradiction type where an essential piece of information (prompt notification) is removed, which has legal implications. The original contract ensured transparency, which is a critical element for compliance with patent law, while the modified version eliminates this crucial protection.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party shall deliver to the other Party, upon request, proof of payment of all such withholding taxes. Each Party shall provide reasonable assistance to the other Party in seeking any benefits available to such Party with respect to government tax withholdings by any relevant law, regulation or double tax treaty.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party may deliver to the other Party, upon request, proof of payment of withholding taxes.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the mandate for 'reasonable assistance', the text removes an obligation for the parties to work together to minimize tax burdens. It could result in either party missing out on potential tax benefits, which can be legally problematic. The original text ensured that both parties would cooperate to optimize tax efficiency, while the modified text introduces the risk of non-compliance with tax regulations or missing opportunities for tax benefits.
perturbation.law_explanation: 26 U.S. Code § 1441 mandates withholding of tax on nonresident aliens. While the modified text still allows for proof of payment, the removal of the 'reasonable assistance' clause hinders the ability of each party to ensure compliance with this requirement and to potentially benefit from tax treaty provisions that could reduce withholding obligations. Therefore, the alteration introduces a risk of non-compliance and potential legal contradictions.

Withholding of tax on nonresident aliens (a) General rule Except as otherwise provided in subsection (c), all persons, in whatever capacity acting (including lessees or mortgagors of real or personal property, fiduciaries, employers, and all officers and employees of the
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 26 U.S. Code § 1441 - Withholding of tax on nonresident aliens | U.S. Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Quick search by citation: Title Section Go! 26 U.S. Code § 1441 - Withholding of tax on nonresident aliens U.S. Code Notes Authorities (CFR) prev | next (a) General rule Except as otherwise provided in subsection (c), all persons, in whatever capacity acting (including lessees or mortgagors of real or personal property, fiduciaries, employers, and all officers and employees of the United States) having the control, receipt, custody, disposal, or payment of any of the items of income specified in subsection (b) (to the extent that any of such items constitutes gross income from sources within the United States), of any nonresident alien individual or of any foreign partnership shall (except as otherwise provided in regulations prescribed by the Secretary under section 874 ) deduct and withhold from such items a tax equal to 30 percent thereof, except that in the case of any item of income specified in the second sentence of subsection (b), the tax shall be equal to 14 percent of such item. (b) Income items The items of income referred to in subsection (a) are interest (other than original issue discount as defined in section 1273), dividends, rent, salaries, wages, premiums, annuities, compensations, remunerations, emoluments, or other fixed or determinable annual or periodical gains, profits, and income, gains described in section 631(b) or (c), amounts subject to tax under section 871(a)(1)(C), and gains subject to tax under section 871(a)(1)(D). The items of income referred to in subsection (a) from which tax shall be deducted and withheld at the rate of 14 percent are amounts which are received by a nonresident alien individual who is temporarily present in the United States as a nonimmigrant under subparagraph (F), (J), (M), or (Q) of section 101(a)(15) of the Immigration and Nationality Act and which are— (1) incid
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates 'reasonable assistance' for tax benefits, but the changed text removes this obligation. This omission contradicts the intent of 26 U.S. Code § 1441, which requires withholding of tax on nonresident aliens, as the 'reasonable assistance' clause could have facilitated compliance with withholding and treaty obligations, potentially reducing tax burdens. By removing this clause, the modified contract introduces a risk of non-compliance, even if it allows for proof of payment.
